subject: The person from the uploaded reference photograph. pose: body angled 20 degrees camera-right with the front shoulder dropped lower than the back shoulder in a lengthened-neck graceful posture, chin pushed slightly forward and angled marginally down to extend the jawline (Hurley turtle technique), upper body leaning approximately one inch toward the lens for face-neck separation and active-listening read, weight shifted onto the back foot in a relaxed natural stance, composed-confident presence with no smile carrying executive register, alive direct eye contact through slightly narrowed lower eyelids (Hurley squinch) for confident no-tension read, a 7 degree natural head tilt toward the brighter raised shoulder, framed mid-chest crop, eye-level camera
